- Reason: `question`

## ev_002

- Source: `paper.md`
- Reason: `question`

> **New to open source or the project?** Start with our [Contributor Onboarding Guide](https://openms.de/contribute/) for a beginner-friendly introduction.

## ev_003

- Source: `paper.md`
- Reason: `missing_information`

You most likely came here to: - report bugs or annoyances - pose questions - point out missing documentation - request new features

## ev_004

- Source: `paper.md`
- Reason: `output`

If you found a bug, e.g. an OpenMS tool crashes during data processing, it is essential to provide some basic information: - the OpenMS version you are running - the platform you are running OpenMS on (Windows 10, ...) - how you installed OpenMS (e.g., from within KNIME, binary installers, self compiled) - a description on how to reproduce the bug - relevant tool output (e.g., error messages) - data to repoduce the bug (If possible as a GitHub gist.

## ev_005

- Source: `paper.md`
- Reason: `workflow`

The severity is usually assigned by OpenMS maintainers and used internally to e.g. indicate if a bug is a blocker for a new release.

## ev_006

- Source: `paper.md`
- Reason: `question`

They might provide additional guidance and valuable information on how your specific aim is achieved.
